tour.title: Getting Started
tour.allDoneDesc: You have completed all the getting-started steps. You can access this tour again from the Help menu at any time.

open_projects.title: Open the Projects window
open_projects.description: From the Workbench Home, open the Projects window to see your saved projects and create new ones. You can also access Projects from the File menu.

create_project.title: Create a new project
create_project.description: Click "Create Project" to start a new saved project, or use the scratch canvas for quick calculations without saving. Each project contains one or more sheets.

add_input.title: Add an input block
add_input.description: Right-click the canvas and choose Insert, or press / for quick insert. Select an input block such as Number Input or Slider to provide values for your computation chain.

add_function.title: Add a function block
add_function.description: Add a function block from the library. Functions cover maths, engineering, finance, and statistics. Browse by category or search by name.

add_output.title: Add an output block
add_output.description: Add a Display block to see the result of your computation. Display blocks show the computed value and can include units.

connect_chains.title: Connect blocks with chains
connect_chains.description: Drag from an output port on one block to an input port on another to create a chain. Chains carry values between blocks. The computation runs automatically.

use_inspector.title: Set values and units in the inspector
use_inspector.description: Click a block to open the inspector window. Here you can set input values, choose units, view descriptions, and check the computed output.

save_project.title: Save your project
save_project.description: Press Ctrl+S (or Cmd+S on Mac) or use the File menu to save. Projects are saved to your account and available across devices.

open_reporting.title: Export reports and notation
open_reporting.description: Pro users can export PDF and Excel reports, view scientific notation for computation chains, and share results. Access these from the File menu.

table_input.title: Import and work with tables
table_input.description: The Table Input block lets you enter or import tabular data (e.g. from CSV). Each column can be connected to downstream blocks or plotted directly. Find it in the Data category of the block library.

problems_panel.title: View errors and warnings
problems_panel.description: The Problems panel (bottom dock) lists all errors and warnings in your canvas — from divide-by-zero to disconnected ports. Click any problem to jump to the affected block.

variables_panel.title: Manage named variables
variables_panel.description: The Variables panel lets you create named variables (like x = 3.14) and bind them to Number or Slider blocks. Change a variable once and every bound block updates automatically.

sheets_tabs.title: Work across multiple sheets
sheets_tabs.description: Each project can have multiple Sheets (canvases). Use the Sheets bar to switch between them, add new ones, or rename and reorder them. Sheets share the same Variables.

ai_copilot.title: Get help from the AI Copilot
ai_copilot.description: The AI Copilot panel (right side) can suggest block chains from natural language, explain your graph, find errors, and more. Click the Sparkles icon or press Ctrl+Shift+A to open it.

welcome.title: Build your first project
welcome.description: This wizard walks you through building a computation chain from scratch. You will pick input blocks, add functions, connect outputs, and learn how to export your results. Each step explains what to do and where to find the tools you need.

pick_inputs.title: Pick your input blocks
pick_inputs.description: Input blocks provide the values your chain works with. Common choices include Number Input (type a value), Slider (drag to set a value), Variable (reference a saved variable), or Constant (physics and maths constants).
pick_inputs.tip: Right-click the canvas and choose Insert > Input Blocks, or press / for quick insert. You can also drag blocks from the Block Library on the left.

pick_functions.title: Pick your function blocks
pick_functions.description: Function blocks perform calculations on your inputs. Browse categories such as Math, Engineering, Finance, and Statistics in the Block Library. Each function shows its formula and expected inputs.
pick_functions.tip: Use the search bar in the Block Library to find functions by name. For example, search for "power" to find the power/work/time function.

pick_outputs.title: Pick your output blocks
pick_outputs.description: Output blocks display the results of your computation chain. Add a Display block to see a single value, or use a Graph block (Pro) to visualise data series.
pick_outputs.tip: Display blocks automatically show the computed value and can include a unit label. You can add multiple displays to show intermediate results.

connect.title: Connect your blocks with chains
connect.description: Drag from an output port (right side of a block) to an input port (left side of another block) to create a chain. Chains carry values between blocks. The computation runs automatically whenever an input changes.
connect.tip: If a chain shows a warning, check that the connected ports expect compatible types. You can delete a chain by clicking it and pressing Delete.

validate.title: Check your results in the inspector
validate.description: Click any block to open the Inspector window. The inspector shows the block's current values, inputs, outputs, and any warnings. Use it to set input values, change units, and verify your computation produces the expected results.
validate.tip: Open the Graph Health panel (View menu) to see a summary of all errors and warnings across your entire canvas.

report.title: Export and share your work
report.description: Save your project with Ctrl+S (Cmd+S on Mac). Pro users can export PDF and Excel reports from the File menu, view scientific notation for computation chains, and share results with colleagues. Free users can save and reopen projects at any time.

engineError.cspTitle: Engine blocked by Content Security Policy
engineError.cspDescription: WebAssembly compilation was blocked by the page's Content Security Policy. If this is the production site, please contact support — this is a server configuration issue.
engineError.mismatchDescription: The compute engine and the app are out of sync. This can happen after an update if your browser is serving a cached version. Clear your browser cache and reload the page.
engineError.genericDescription: The compute engine could not initialize. This may be caused by a browser extension blocking WebAssembly or a network issue.
